site.name: isready.ai
site.tagline: Is your website ready for AI?
site.description: Free open-source audit: is your site readable, crawlable and optimized for ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity and every AI search engine? Score it in ~5 seconds.

welcome.title: You're in. Let's get your site AI-ready.
welcome.subtitle: isready.ai scores how readable, crawlable and quotable your website is for ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity and every AI engine — then shows you exactly what to fix.
welcome.bulletsTitle: What you can do from here
welcome.bulletScanBody: Audit a page against dozens of agent-readability checks in seconds.
welcome.bulletTrackBody: Keep scores, history and badges for every site in one workspace.
welcome.bulletFixBody: Work through a concrete resolution for each finding, then re-scan.

onboarding.title: Welcome to IsReady.AI — let's make your site agent-ready
onboarding.subtitle: Here's everything you can do from here.

stepScan.title: Run a scan
stepScan.body: Audit any URL against 32 agent-readability checks across 5 dimensions, including a real-browser agent check.

stepTrack.title: Track a site
stepTrack.body: Add a site to your workspace to keep its score, history and badge in one place.

stepReport.title: Read the report
stepReport.body: See exactly what passed, what failed and why — with a shareable report for your team.

stepFix.title: Fix the issues
stepFix.body: Work through a concrete resolution for every finding and re-scan to confirm the fix.

smart.kicker: A real browser, not a basic bot
smart.title: Smart agent readability
smart.subtitle: AI agents now browse, click and buy on behalf of real people — on WordPress, Shopify and custom stacks alike. Smart agent readability drives a real browser-based agent through your pages and scores, 0–100, whether it can truly see your content, follow the layout and complete the actions that matter, like search and checkout. And it's only getting smarter: soon the same agent runs every isready.ai check for you, then fixes what it can and hands you a ready-to-paste prompt for ChatGPT, Claude Code or any LLM to finish the job. Pro turns every failure into a concrete, copy-paste fix.
smart.viewBody: the exact rendered accessibility tree, named controls and navigation exposed to the agent.
smart.askBody: the Pro report assistant, opened with ⌘I, grounded in both scores and the actual Smart Agent View.
smart.demoQuestion: What could confuse an agent here?
smart.demoAnswer: The page is readable, but two icon-only controls have no accessible name. Label them so an agent can identify their purpose before acting.

how.title: How it works
how.subtitle: Grounded in how AI crawlers actually behave — not SEO folklore.
how.step1Body: We request your pages exactly like GPTBot and ClaudeBot do: raw HTTP, no JavaScript, short timeouts, honest user-agent.
how.step2Body: We parse the raw HTML exactly as GPTBot and ClaudeBot do — no JavaScript, because they don't run it either. Empty app shells and content that only appears after hydration get flagged.
how.step3Body: 32 evidence-based checks across 5 dimensions produce a 0–100 score, and every finding ships with a concrete fix, impact and effort.

checks.title: What we check
checks.subtitle: Every check is sourced from official crawler docs and peer-reviewed GEO research.
checks.crawlerBody: robots.txt per AI bot (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot…), Cloudflare anti-bot challenges, redirects, TTFB, noindex.
checks.renderingBody: Empty app shells, missing semantic landmarks, scripts with no server-rendered text, noscript fallbacks.
checks.structuredBody: JSON-LD presence and types, meta basics, Open Graph, author & E-E-A-T signals, lang attributes.
checks.trustBody: HTTPS enforcement, TLS validity, HSTS, mixed content.
checks.geoBody: Content depth, heading structure, statistics & citations — the signals that raise visibility in generative answers.
checks.llmsBody: llms.txt and Content Signals, reported honestly as informational: server logs show AI crawlers fetch llms.txt in ~0.1% of visits — we never score it.

cli.title: Audit from your terminal
cli.subtitle: The entire scanner ships as an open-source CLI — every check, no install, no signup. One command audits your whole site, `--json` feeds your tooling, `--llm` writes the fix plan, and the exit code fails your CI the moment your score drops. Your full AI-readiness gate, in a single line.
cli.heroOr: or run it in your terminal

gh.point1: Score gate — exit 1 below your threshold

gh.point3: score and grade outputs for downstream steps
gh.point4: Free to run in any GitHub workflow
gh.badgeText: Served live by isready.ai for your verified domain (Pro & Team) and re-scanned on a schedule — it stays current on its own, no CI step. Copy the Markdown into your README.
gh.viewOnGithubAria: View on GitHub (opens in a new tab)

why.title: Why AI readiness matters now
why.p1: AI assistants are becoming a primary discovery channel. Cloudflare measured AI crawlers at roughly 50 billion requests per day in 2025 — nearly 1% of all web traffic — and GPTBot traffic grew over 300% year-over-year. Yet the rules are different from classic SEO: GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot and OAI-SearchBot read raw HTML only and never execute JavaScript, while Googlebot renders everything. A React or Vue app with client-side rendering can rank on page one of Google and still be a blank page to every AI assistant.
why.p2: The research is equally clear about content. The peer-reviewed GEO study (Aggarwal et al., KDD 2024) tested 10,000 queries across multiple generative engines and found that adding quotations improved visibility in AI answers by 41%, statistics by 32%, and citing credible sources by up to 115% for lower-ranked sites. As the authors put it:
why.quote: Generative engines synthesize answers from a handful of sources — the competition is no longer for a ranking position, but for being part of the answer itself.
why.p3: Meanwhile, one misconfigured header can erase you: Cloudflare's AI-bot blocking now protects millions of sites, and many owners don't realize it also removes them from ChatGPT and Perplexity answers. Independent server-log studies show 2.5 million+ websites already disallow AI training — a legitimate choice, but one that should be made deliberately, not by accident. That is exactly what this scanner makes visible: 32 evidence-based checks, each with the observed value, the consequence, and the precise fix.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions

crawler.question: What does Crawler access measure?
crawler.purpose: Whether AI crawlers can reach, fetch and index the site without being denied, challenged, redirected excessively or served a degraded response.
crawler.method: The scanner fetches robots.txt and the page with standard and AI-specific user agents, then checks bot directives, anti-bot responses, HTTP status, redirects, noindex and snippet restrictions, sitemap discovery, host consistency and response time.

rendering.question: What does Rendering measure?
rendering.purpose: Whether the useful page content exists in the raw server response read by non-JavaScript AI crawlers, rather than appearing only after client-side rendering.
rendering.method: The scanner parses the returned HTML and measures visible text, semantic main content, empty application shells, noscript fallbacks, image alternatives and optional Markdown content negotiation.

structured.question: What does Structured data measure?
structured.purpose: Whether machines can identify the page, its subject, language, canonical URL, publisher and content type without inferring them from presentation alone.
structured.method: The scanner parses JSON-LD and validates useful identity and content types, then checks title, description, canonical URL, Open Graph metadata, author and E-E-A-T signals, lang and hreflang declarations.

trust.question: What does Trust & security measure?
trust.purpose: Whether an AI system can fetch and cite the page over a valid, consistently secure connection.
trust.method: The scanner verifies HTTPS and TLS validity, checks the Strict-Transport-Security header and detects insecure HTTP sub-resources embedded in an HTTPS page.

geo.question: What does Content (GEO) measure?
geo.purpose: Whether the page contains substantial, clear and evidence-backed material that a generative engine can extract and cite in an answer.
geo.method: The scanner evaluates content depth, heading hierarchy, statistics, quotations and external citations, content-to-navigation ratio, machine-readable freshness and answer-friendly structures such as lists, tables and question-shaped sections.

smartVisible.question: What does Visible content (Smart Agent) measure?
smartVisible.purpose: Whether a browser-capable agent actually perceives real content in the rendered accessibility tree, rather than an empty shell, a loading state or a challenge page.
smartVisible.method: After the page renders, the agent reads the accessibility snapshot and measures perceivable text nodes, the amount of content exposed and whether images carry a text alternative.

smartStructure.question: What does Understandable structure (Smart Agent) measure?
smartStructure.purpose: Whether the rendered page exposes a clear semantic structure an agent can reason about — landmarks and a sensible heading hierarchy.
smartStructure.method: The agent inspects the snapshot for ARIA landmarks (banner, navigation, main, contentinfo) and a well-formed heading outline with a single h1 and ordered subheadings.

smartContent.question: What does Content quality (Smart Agent) measure?
smartContent.purpose: Whether the rendered content is substantive and self-describing — a meaningful title, enough depth and elements that carry accessible names.
smartContent.method: The agent evaluates the page title, the depth of the rendered content and the share of elements exposed with an accessible name rather than anonymous nodes.

smartControls.question: What does Accessible controls (Smart Agent) measure?
smartControls.purpose: Whether the interactive controls an agent must operate — links, buttons, inputs — expose accessible names it can target.
smartControls.method: The agent collects the interactive elements from the snapshot and measures how many carry a non-empty accessible name versus unlabelled controls.

smartNavigability.question: What does Navigability (Smart Agent) measure?
smartNavigability.purpose: Whether a browser-capable agent can find its way around — navigation landmarks and links that let it move between sections and pages.
smartNavigability.method: The agent checks the snapshot for navigation landmarks and the presence of usable links that expose the site's structure.

smartBarriers.question: What does Agent barriers (Smart Agent) measure?
smartBarriers.purpose: Whether anything blocks a browser-capable agent from reaching the content — bot challenges, cookie walls, 'just a moment' interstitials or login gates.
smartBarriers.method: The agent scans the rendered title and snapshot for known interstitial and challenge patterns that would stop it before the real content loads.

faq.q1: Do AI crawlers and AI agents see the same page?
faq.a1: No. GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot and OAI-SearchBot generally read raw HTML without rendering your interface. Browser-capable agents can execute JavaScript and operate controls. We measure these separately as Agent Readability and Smart agent readability.
faq.a2: The standard score is a weighted 0–100 across five crawler and content dimensions. Smart agent readability is a separate 0–100 across visible content, understandable structure, content quality, accessible controls, navigability and browser barriers. Both are versioned and evidence-based.
faq.a3: Yes — the scanner engine and CLI are MIT-licensed open source, the web scan is free, and running npx isreadyai gives you the full audit plus a resolution plan (--llm) at no cost. The hosted dashboard itself is proprietary (source-available under PolyForm Shield, © Smart Squad S.r.l.), not MIT. Pro and Team add the web resolution, live domain badges, Ask your site, monitoring, scheduled re-scans, report history and automated AI fix pull requests.
faq.q4: Can you scan apps behind a login?
faq.a4: Public pages only for now. Scanning login-gated SaaS via GitHub-connected source is on the roadmap — vote on it in the repo.
faq.q5: Does llms.txt help AI crawlers find my content?
faq.a5: It can't hurt, but don't expect miracles: no major AI provider has confirmed consuming it, and Google has said it doesn't use it. We report it as informational and never let it affect your score.
faq.q6: Can I run this in CI or from the terminal?
faq.a6: Yes. Run npx isreadyai yourdomain.com for the full audit in your terminal, with --json for machines and --llm for a fix plan you can paste into an AI coding agent. The GitHub Action gates deployments on a minimum score and writes the full report to the job summary.
faq.a7: Your customers ask ChatGPT, Perplexity and Gemini before they ask Google. If AI can't read your site, you're invisible in the answers they trust — no matter how well you rank on classic search. isready.ai shows you exactly what every AI sees and the precise fixes to get cited.

report.title: AI readiness report
report.scanningHint: Fetching like an AI crawler and running {count} checks…
report.aiSearchDescription: Whether AI search and answer engines can crawl, parse and cite this page. Citation crawlers (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, Perplexity) read raw HTML — not JavaScript — so this scores the signals that actually earn citations: clean structure, content depth, statistics and structured data.
report.scoreExplainerBody: Your headline score is the average of two tracks. AI Search measures whether AI search and answer engines can crawl, parse and cite you — citation crawlers like GPTBot, ClaudeBot and Perplexity read raw HTML, not JavaScript.
report.scoreExplainerBody2: AI Smart Agent measures whether a browser-capable agent can perceive and use the page once it has rendered.

admin.compareDescription: See what changed between two scans of the same site.
admin.title: Overview
admin.description: Your scans, domains, API keys and account settings in one place.
admin.dataPending: Connect to the admin data layer
admin.recentActivityHint: Scans and operational events will appear here.
admin.emptyActivity: No admin data source is connected yet.
admin.foundationHint: The structural pieces available for the next admin features.
admin.foundationComponents: Reusable HeroUI cards, breadcrumbs and metrics
admin.foundationAccess: Environment gate ready to be replaced by authenticated access
admin.scansDescription: Inspect queued, running, completed and failed scans from a single operational view.
admin.scansEmpty: Connect the scans table and filters to the Supabase admin data layer.
admin.domainsDescription: Track your websites, verify ownership, and monitor their AI-readiness over time.
admin.domainsEmpty: No claimed domains yet — add one to start monitoring.
admin.apiKeysDescription: Create, revoke and inspect usage for product API keys without exposing raw secrets.
admin.apiKeysEmpty: Connect API key issuance and metering before enabling this page.
admin.settingsDescription: Centralize operational defaults, quotas and feature availability.
admin.settingsEmpty: No admin-configurable settings are available yet.

contact.metaDescription: Get in touch with the isready.ai team: send feedback, report a bug, or report a fraudulent domain claim.
contact.title: Get in touch
contact.intro: Send us feedback or a bug report — or, if a domain that's yours has been verified by someone else, report the fraudulent claim and we'll investigate.
contact.hostHint: The exact domain that's yours and was claimed by someone else.
contact.successBody: Thanks — we've received it and will get back to you by email.
contact.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
contact.unconfigured: Contact is temporarily unavailable. Please email us instead.
